_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'waterway blocked since February 2026' → 'late-February 2026 conflict; Hormuz traffic collapsed' (avoid implying continuous total blockade).
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'fertiliser raw materials surged 100-150%' specific range → World Bank 'sharp March 2026 fertiliser price spike'.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Hari Raya price controls on 12 vegetables ended 28 March' → '14-28 March festive maximum price scheme covering 27 essential items'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Hook 'Food prices are rising because of the Middle East conflict' framed with multi-causal acknowledgement (diesel, weather, supply chain).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — 'Malaysia has no stockpile and no domestic production' → 'Malaysia imports over 60% of mineral-fertiliser requirements; we could not find a documented stabilisation list or public-stock reporting mechanism'.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — '23 fertiliser vessels blocked' → 'Kpler observed 23 fertiliser-related vessels in the Gulf with Hormuz transit uncertain'.

REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Nitrogen fertiliser RM240→RM305/25kg → RM350 by mid-April' specific series → 'Federation of Vegetable Farmers Association said input costs expected to rise materially'.
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Wholesaler margins 30-50%' and 'kangkung 30sen/RM3' (no farmgate-to-retail dataset).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — 'B40 spend 40%+ of income; T20 15%' → DOSM expenditure shares framing (not income).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'rounding error / skipped meal' → 'lower-income households more exposed; coping behaviour requires separate evidence'.
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'Women manage 80% of household food purchases' (no Malaysian survey).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Invisible in inflation stats, visible in child nutrition' → 'distributional harm can be obscured in aggregate CPI'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'never stockpiled a single bag' rhetorical → 'no documented stabilisation list or public-stock reporting mechanism'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — View 'palm-oil priorities, middlemen taking more than farmers' specific causal claims softened to 'lacks the buffers Indonesia, Vietnam, Thailand have built'.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'War made it visible; policy made it inevitable' framed as analytical view rather than factual conclusion.
CLEARED legacyAudit (rebuild complete per CLAUDE.md Accuracy Standard).

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): One-third global fertiliser through Hormuz; farmers warn 30-50% hikes; Indonesia Pupuk stockpile; Vietnam price law covers fertiliser; Thailand monitors fertiliser reserves.
